claim:
She comes in with abdominal pain of less than 1
days duration.
evidence_preview: Having abdominal pain with turning and repositioning.

claim:
She describes the pain as sharp and epigastric
and band-like in nature.
evidence_preview: Pain:Pt states she was having severe pain medicated with MS 2mg IVP with relief down to no pain.

claim:
She has had some nausea with a few
episodes of vomiting without signs of hematemesis.

claim:
She was
found by social working writhing in pain at her group home.
evidence_preview: Pain:Pt states she was having severe pain medicated with MS 2mg IVP with relief down to no pain.

claim:
She, of note, has a long history of constipation but no
recent diarrhea.

Note is made of
 air outlining the thoracic aorta representing mediastinal emphysema likely

claim:
She reports no chest pain, shortness of
breath or other associated symptoms.

claim:
Otherwise, had been
taking a normal diet for the last 2 days.

claim:
PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:  Significant for hyperlipidemia,
hypertension; history of ventricular fibrillation with
cardiac arrest.
evidence_preview:
Mediastinal emphysema likely secondary to the patient's known history of
 perforation of the high rectum and exploratory laparotomy.

claim: She has a converting device in place.
evidence_preview:
A right internal jugular venous catheter has been pulled
 back and the tip now resides in the mid SVC.

claim: Status post cholecystectomy.
evidence_preview: Remains broad coverage post operative.

claim:
Obsessive compulsive disorder,
bipolar disorder and B-12 deficiency with a questionable
allergy to sulfa and Zoloft.
evidence_preview: Neuro:  Sedated on Propofol, easily arousable to voice, Hx of bipolar disorder and OCD.

claim:
PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:  On admission, temperature was 99.4
Fahrenheit; heart rate 84; blood pressure 108/64; respiratory
rate 20 and breathing 97% on room air.
evidence_preview: Sinus rhythm, rate 83.

claim:
General: She was a
moderately distressed elderly appearing female.
evidence_preview: Pain:Pt states she was having severe pain medicated with MS 2mg IVP with relief down to no pain.

claim:
She was
normocephalic, atraumatic.
evidence_preview: Pain:Pt states she was having severe pain medicated with MS 2mg IVP with relief down to no pain.

claim:
Pupils were equally round and
reactive to light.
evidence_preview: Neuro:Pt oriented to year and name otherwise very confused, MAE spontaneously and equally.

claim: Extraocular movements intact.
evidence_preview: Skin intact on backside.

claim:
Oropharynx
was clear and mucous membranes were moist.
evidence_preview: GI: Ileostomy and mucous fistula present.

claim:
Neck was supple
with no lymphadenopathy.
evidence_preview: Pain:Pt states she was having severe pain medicated with MS 2mg IVP with relief down to no pain.

claim:
No masses or thyromegaly were

evidence_preview: No phone calls or visitor this evening.

claim:
Heart was regular rate and rhythm with no
murmur, rub or gallop.
evidence_preview: Sinus rhythm, rate 83.

claim:
Normal S1 and S2, clear to
auscultation bilaterally without wheezes, rales or rhonchi.
evidence_preview: The aorta is of normal caliber.

claim:
Abdomen was firm, diffusely tender with hypoactive bowel
sounds and was severely distended.
evidence_preview: Barium rectal contrast was administered.

claim:
There were no signs of any
rebound or guarding at this time and there were no masses
palpated on exam.
evidence_preview: BONE WINDOWS:  There are no suspicious osteolytic or sclerotic lesions.

claim: There was no hepatosplenomegaly.
evidence_preview: BONE WINDOWS:  There are no suspicious osteolytic or sclerotic lesions.

claim:
Extremity
exam revealed no clubbing, cyanosis or edema.
evidence_preview: No phone calls or visitor this evening.

claim:
Neurologic
exam revealed the patient to be alert and oriented to person
and place but not time and occasionally seemed confused and
disturbed by the situation at hand.
evidence_preview: Alert and oriented to self.

claim:
Her strength was [**3-31**]
throughout and her sensation was normal throughout.
evidence_preview: Pulses palpable throughout.

claim:
HOSPITAL COURSE:  At this time, the patient was further
evaluated in the emergency department.
evidence_preview: This info was passed on to the ICU team and social worker.

claim:
A kidney, ureter,
bladder film was performed that revealed marked dilation of
the descending and transverse colon to 10.7 cm and then a
distinct transition point in the descending colon.
evidence_preview:
CT OF THE PELVIS WITHOUT IV CONTRAST:  There is rectal contrast noted,
 extending from the rectum to a portion of the descending colon.

claim:
There was
no free air and no dilated loops of small bowel.
evidence_preview: Small bowel loops are unremarkable.

claim:
There was no
air in the rectum.
evidence_preview:
There is a large amount of retroperitoneal
  and intraperitoneal free air, and there appears to be perforation at the right
  side of the high rectum.

claim:
At this point, cecal volvulus was
considered primarily with question of perforation.
evidence_preview: Findings suggestive of cecal volvulus.

claim:
There was
also free air on this film under the diaphragm on the right
side, tracking down to the hip.
evidence_preview:
There is a large amount of retroperitoneal
  and intraperitoneal free air, and there appears to be perforation at the right
  side of the high rectum.

claim:
Also at this time, a CAT
scan was performed.
evidence_preview:
The kidneys
 excrete contrast appropriately (30 cc of IV contrast was given prior to the
 scan).

claim:
An attempt was made to decompress with a
rigid sigmoidoscope.
evidence_preview:
Resp Care

Pt remains intubated and currently vented on A/C with no changes made to parameter settings.

claim: This was unsuccessful.
evidence_preview: This info was passed on to the ICU team and social worker.

claim:
This was done by
the gastroenterology service; thus, the patient required an
emergent trip to the operating room.
evidence_preview: This info was passed on to the ICU team and social worker.

claim:
On the CAT scan, there
was also notable free air retroperitoneal and subcutaneous
and a cecal volvulus with colon up to 15 cm.
evidence_preview:
There is a large amount of retroperitoneal
 free air surrounding the kidneys and aorta.

claim:
The patient was
consented and brought to the operating room urgently after
intravenous fluids and antibiotics were instituted.
evidence_preview:
The findings were immediately discussed with the surgical and GI services
 involved in the patient's care.

claim:
Under
general anesthesia, the procedure was performed which
included a right colectomy, a repair of a sigmoid
perforation, primarily an end ileostomy and an end transverse
colostomy.
evidence_preview:
There is a massively distended loop of colon within the
 right lower quadrant, with an air stool level.

claim:
The patient tolerated the procedure well under
general anesthesia and received 4500 ml of Crystalloid.
evidence_preview: Patient tolerated cpap poorly when off propofol.

claim:
She
had a urine output of 345 ml during the case.
evidence_preview: Urine output decreased and drk amber.

claim:
A drain was
also placed at this time and her estimated total blood loss
was 250 ml.
evidence_preview: This info was passed on to the ICU team and social worker.

claim:
The right colon, at this time, was sent for
pathology.
evidence_preview: This info was passed on to the ICU team and social worker.

claim:
In the immediate postoperative period, the patient's pain was
fairly well controlled.
evidence_preview:
The findings were immediately discussed with the surgical and GI services
 involved in the patient's care.

claim:
Her sedation was weaned and she was
extubated on postoperative day number 2.
evidence_preview: Barium rectal contrast was administered.

claim:
She was placed on
Metoprolol at this time and she was continued on intravenous
fluids and antibiotics.
evidence_preview: This info was passed on to the ICU team and social worker.
